sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Valencia are unbeaten in their last 4 matches, showing resilience with draws and a narrow win, though they often concede first. Sevilla, on the other hand, have struggled, losing 3 of their last 5 matches, with defensive issues evident as they've failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Valencia have held the edge, securing a 1-0 win and a 1-1 draw. Matches between them tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 7 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Valencia (16th, 14 points) and Sevilla (14th, 16 points) are closely matched in the LaLiga standings, both struggling offensively with 13 and 19 goals scored respectively. Defensively, both have conceded 22-23 goals, indicating vulnerabilities at the back.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Valencia's Luis Rioja has been a standout against Sevilla historically, with an average Sofascore rating of 7.58, while Sevilla miss key players like Ruben Vargas, impacting their attacking threat. Sevilla's goalkeeper Ørjan Nyland is in form, potentially crucial in a tight match.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's probabilities (Valencia 40%, Draw 25%, Sevilla 35%) and current form, Valencia might have a slight edge at home in Mestalla. However, Sevilla's desperation for points could make this a competitive, low-scoring affair.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Valencia and Sevilla at Mestalla ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Both teams showed intent but lacked the cutting edge in front of goal. Sevilla held a slight edge in possession and created more dangerous opportunities, while Valencia struggled to convert their entries into the final third into clear-cut chances. Discipline-wise, Sevilla picked up a yellow card, while Valencia played a cleaner game without bookings. The match remains finely poised as both sides head into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla started the game with stronger attacking momentum, dominating the early minutes with consistent pressure in the opposition half. Their attacking spells were frequent and sustained during the first 20 minutes, showing intent to control the game. However, Valencia responded with brief periods of momentum around the middle of the half, pushing forward in bursts to challenge Sevilla's defense. As the half progressed, momentum shifted back to Sevilla with several dangerous forays, though they couldn’t capitalize. Towards the end of the half, Valencia mounted a late surge with multiple attacking spells, suggesting they could carry some confidence into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla showed more promise in attack during the first half, evidenced by creating a big chance, though they missed it. They also recorded a shot on target and earned more corner kicks compared to Valencia. Their higher percentage of duels won, especially in the air, indicates a physical edge over the home side. Valencia, on the other hand, had more entries into the penalty area but failed to create significant opportunities from these positions. Both teams were close in terms of final third entries, but Sevilla made better use of their moments with possession won in dangerous areas. The expected goals metric also leans in Sevilla’s favor, underlining their slight superiority in creating scoring chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Valencia, José Copete stood out defensively with a high number of clearances and contributions in duels, earning a strong rating. Arnaut Danjuma also showed promise in attack with some involvement in forward play. On Sevilla’s side, Nemanja Gudelj was impressive at the back with key clearances and a solid presence in duels, reflected in his high rating. Oso made an impact creatively, being involved in setting up a big chance, while Akor Adams, despite missing a big opportunity, remains a threat due to his positioning and involvement in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Sevilla push harder to convert their first-half dominance into a goal, especially given their missed big chance and higher expected goals. They may look to exploit Valencia’s lack of shots on target by maintaining their physical edge and pressing high. Valencia, however, will aim to capitalize on their late first-half momentum and increase their threat in the final third, possibly through better utilization of players like Danjuma. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, with Valencia potentially focusing on tighter defending while seeking counter-attacking opportunities. Sevilla’s discipline will need monitoring after their first-half booking, as another card could shift the balance. Overall, the game is expected to open up as both teams chase a breakthrough.
